codeReview.summary: Clean extraction of 6 rendering modules from compositor into libraries/render/. The render library is correctly structured as a no_std rlib with proper extern crate dependencies (drawing, scene, protocol, fonts) and zero dependency on sys or ipc. Module contents are byte-for-byte identical to compositor originals, with only three justified changes: (1) extern crate alloc moved to lib.rs, (2) round_f32/scale_coord/scale_size made pub for re-export, (3) scale_size_u16 added at crate root as a pub helper. RenderBackend trait has correct signature. CpuBackend struct implements the trait with render() delegating to render_scene_with_pool and dirty_rects() returning damage rects. Build.rs correctly compiles the rlib with all 4 extern dependencies and font search paths. Test crate migrated from #[path] includes to render:: imports. All 1,764 tests pass per handoff verification. Compositor retains its old code as specified — removal happens in the next feature.

issues.description: All CpuBackend fields are pub. While pragmatic for a no_std crate (avoids builder boilerplate), this exposes internal state (glyph caches, damage tracker, surface pool) directly. The RenderBackend trait provides the clean interface, but callers can bypass it by reaching into pub fields. Consider making fields pub(crate) in a follow-up if the compositor only accesses CpuBackend through the trait after wiring.

CpuBackend has no constructor (new/from) method at this commit — fields must be initialized manually by the caller. This is acceptable since the next feature (wire-compositor-to-render-library) handles construction, but a new() method would improve ergonomics and enforce invariants.

The RenderBackend::render() impl at commit d84f4c8 always calls render_scene_with_pool unconditionally (no damage-aware clipping). The later commit 1497dc2 adds prepare_frame/finish_frame and damage-aware rendering (full vs clipped). At this commit, the trait impl is correct but damage information is not used in the render path — acceptable since this is the extraction step and damage-aware rendering is wired in the next feature.

VAL-BUILD-001 states 'The compositor's build step includes --extern render=...' but at this commit the compositor does not yet have --extern render (expected per feature description). The first half of VAL-BUILD-001 (render rlib compilation) is satisfied. The second half belongs to the wire-compositor-to-render-library feature.

sharedStateObservations.description: Worker's skillFeedback suggests the refactor-worker skill should mention creating Cargo.toml for new libraries (needed for the test crate's Cargo-based dependency resolution, separate from the bare-metal build.rs flow). This is a valid gap — the skill's procedure covers build.rs and test crate updates but doesn't mention the Cargo.toml file needed for host-side compilation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The refactor-worker skill could mention that when creating a new library that test files reference, you also need to create a Cargo.toml for the library (since the test crate uses Cargo for dependency resolution, separate from the bare-metal build.rs flow).'

sharedStateObservations.description: build-system.md was already updated with render library position in the rlib chain (step 8) and compositor submodule tracking notes. This is good practice and no gap exists here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: build-system.md contains: '8. `render` (depends on drawing, scene, protocol, fonts search paths) — **Added in Phase 1 extract**'

summary: PASS. The create-render-library feature is a clean, well-executed extraction. All 6 rendering modules (scene_render.rs, svg.rs, compositing.rs, surface_pool.rs, damage.rs, cursor.rs) were copied into libraries/render/ with minimal and correct modifications (visibility changes, extern crate alloc consolidation). The RenderBackend trait and CpuBackend struct are properly defined. Build.rs integration is correct with proper extern flags and rerun-if-changed directives. Test crate successfully migrated from #[path] compositor includes to render:: library imports. No sys/ipc dependencies. All 1,764 tests pass. The compositor retains its old code as designed for the two-phase extraction approach. Four non-blocking observations noted: pub field visibility, missing constructor, damage-unaware initial render impl (all addressed by the next feature), and partial VAL-BUILD-001 fulfillment (compositor --extern render deferred to next feature).
